_comment: Official Yashil Makon (Green Space) national programme figures, curated manually from government and press sources (each milestone links to its source). Update this file as new official numbers are announced - nothing here is generated.

milestones.title: Uzbekistan to restore 1.27 million hectares of forests and develop a 'desert economy'
milestones.description: The 2026-2030 plan: new and restored forests on 1.27 million hectares plus 16,000 hectares of protective forest belts in desert, mountain and foothill zones - with green cover on the dried Aral seabed reaching 2.5 million hectares (78%) by the end of 2026.

milestones.title: President plants the first trees of the National Dendrological Park
milestones.description: A flagship 2026 addition to the programme: a national dendrological park designed to hold thousands of tree species and anchor Tashkent's green belt.

milestones.title: Yashil Makon expands as a foundation of green growth
milestones.description: The Ministry of Ecology on scaling the programme beyond the original 200-million-saplings-a-year target: more than a billion trees and shrubs planted nationwide, lifting green cover from 8% in 2020 to 14.3% in 2025.

milestones.title: National Dendrological Park project launched
milestones.description: The presidential announcement of the park project - the official record of one of the programme's most visible 2026 initiatives.
